{"id":"beefcake-swarm-a8m","title":"Add merge conflict test for WorktreeBridge","description":"WorktreeBridge tests cover create and list but not the merge conflict scenario, which is the most operationally dangerous path. When the main branch moves forward with a conflicting change while the worktree branch also makes changes, merge_and_remove() will fail — and the cleanup behavior needs to be verified.

Fix: Add test case to worktree_bridge.rs::tests:
1. Create a worktree
2. Commit a change to a file on the main branch
3. Commit a conflicting change to the same file in the worktree
4. Call merge_and_remove() — assert it returns Err
5. Verify the worktree still exists (not accidentally deleted)
6. Call remove_worktree() — assert it cleans up (depends on worktree cleanup method)

Also test: merge_and_remove() with uncommitted changes (should fail with descriptive error).

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-10m","title":"Upgrade ContextPacker to span-aware error-driven retrieval (Potpie pattern)","description":"All 4 models identified that current context packing (first 30 lines per file) misses critical information. Steal Potpie pattern: on verifier failures, use rustc error spans to retrieve relevant code regions. Specifically: (1) Parse rustc JSON error spans (file + line range), (2) Pack ±80 lines around each error span, (3) Include trait definitions and impl blocks referenced in trait bound errors, (4) Include callers/callees of symbols in error messages. Depends on tree-sitter integration for symbol graph. Files: coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs (pack_retry enhancement), coordination/src/verifier/report.rs (span extraction from rustc JSON). Depends on tree-sitter issue.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:49:14Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-16T13:02:59Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-9fs","title":"Short-circuit manager timeout when worker completes (rig agent opacity)","description":"The manager agent has a 45-minute timeout. When a worker finishes in 8 minutes (job 1629), the manager still runs until timeout because rig's agent-as-tool pattern doesn't expose intermediate completion signals. Options: (1) Reduce AGENT_TIMEOUT to 15min since most real work happens in first 10min, (2) Monitor worktree for git changes and short-circuit when changes + verifier pass detected, (3) Add a side-channel (file flag) that the verifier sets when green. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs","notes":"MANUAL: Requires async signal handling and understanding of rig agent internals. {"id":"beefcake-swarm-1nt","title":"Add ast-grep scan as warning gate in verifier pipeline","description":"We now have 7 ast-grep rules in rules/. {"id":"beefcake-gds","title":"Fix retry context collapse: include full changed/failing file content in pack_retry","description":"CRITICAL: pack_retry currently produces only ~300-700 tokens with 1 file header. Iteration 2+ in job 1653 had 727→308→356 tokens vs iteration 1's 23,805 tokens. The worker literally cannot see the code it needs to fix. Immediate fix: (1) Always include FULL content of files with verifier errors, (2) Include full content of files modified since initial commit, (3) Include compact attempt memory (error→action→result tuples). No tree-sitter or span-awareness needed. This is a minimal pack_retry fix. Files: coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs, coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-18T03:19:09Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-18T03:19:09Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-mu1","title":"Implement graceful shutdown with worktree cleanup","description":"The orchestrator has no signal handler. If killed via SIGINT (Ctrl+C) or SIGTERM (deployment, systemd stop) while a worktree is active, the worktree remains on disk and registered in git, causing disk space leaks and 'already checked out' errors on restart.

Fix: Use tokio::signal::ctrl_c() and tokio::signal::unix::signal(SignalKind::terminate()) to catch shutdown signals. When received:
1. Set a shutdown flag (AtomicBool or tokio::sync::watch)
2. Check the flag at the top of each loop iteration
3. On shutdown: log the in-progress issue ID, call worktree_bridge.remove_worktree(), update beads status back to 'open', then exit cleanly

Consider wrapping the active worktree path in an Arc<Mutex<Option<String>>> so the signal handler knows what to clean up. Requires the remove_worktree() method from the worktree cleanup issue.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/main.rs
Depends on: worktree cleanup method (beefcake-swarm-rb2)

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-5bk","title":"Integrate tree-sitter-rust for AST-aware context packing","description":"Replace regex/line-based symbol extraction in ContextPacker with tree-sitter-rust AST parsing. Currently build_file_contexts() takes first 30 lines of each file (packer.rs:94-136) which misses critical context. {"id":"beefcake-swarm-j4l","title":"Add Planner agent with structured change contracts","description":"UNANIMOUS CONSENSUS (4/4 models: G3-Pro, GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.2, Opus 4.5)

The current loop goes straight to code generation without formalizing intent. Add a Planner agent (can use the fast 14B model) that runs BEFORE the Implementer and produces a structured 'change contract':

- Acceptance criteria (what must be true when done)
- Invariants / behavioral promises (what must NOT change)
- Files expected to change (scoping)
- Risk classification (API break risk, concurrency risk, security risk)
- Test plan: which tests to add/modify, what failure proves correctness

The contract becomes:
1. A constraint for the Implementer (bounded search space)
2. A checklist for the Validator (check against plan, not just 'does it look right')
3. An audit trail (what was intended vs what happened)
4. Partial rollback points (if step 3/5 fails, retry from step 3)

Implementation: Add a PlannerAgent trait and struct in swarm-agents, produce a ChangeContract type in coordination/. The contract feeds into WorkPacket.constraints and WorkPacket.decisions. The Validator receives the contract alongside the diff.

G3-Pro calls this 'Legislator-Executor'. GPT-5.2-Codex calls it '3-stage contract + selection'. Both identify it as the highest-impact architectural change.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/ (new planner module), coordination/src/ (new contract types), crates/swarm-agents/src/main.rs","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:34:21Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-16T13:03:00Z","labels":["architecture"]}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-7go","title":"Detect and clean zombie branches on startup","description":"If the orchestrator crashes mid-loop, /tmp may clean the worktree directory but the swarm/<issue_id> branch persists in the repo. On next run, git worktree add -b fails with 'A branch named swarm/... already exists' and the agent gets permanently stuck on that issue.

Fix: On startup (before the main loop), scan for orphaned swarm/* branches that have no corresponding worktree directory. For each orphan: attempt git branch -D to delete it. Also check git worktree list --porcelain for stale entries and run git worktree prune. This requires the remove_worktree() method from the worktree cleanup issue.

Add a WorktreeBridge::cleanup_stale() method that:
1. Runs git worktree prune
2. Lists all branches matching swarm/*
3. For each, checks if worktree_path(id) exists
4. If not, force-deletes the branch

Call cleanup_stale() in main() before entering the issue-picking loop.

{"description":"WorkPacketGenerator::git_branch() in coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs (line 394) uses 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD' which returns the literal string 'HEAD' when in detached HEAD state (common in CI, after checkout of a specific commit, or in freshly created worktrees before the first commit).

This means the WorkPacket.branch field becomes 'HEAD' which is misleading in the prompt sent to the LLM.

FIX in git_branch() method (line 394-402):
1. If result == 'HEAD', check CI env vars: CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, GITHUB_HEAD_REF, BRANCH_NAME
2. If still 'HEAD', try 'git name-rev --name-only HEAD' and strip remotes/ prefix
3. If still 'HEAD', compute 'detached@<short-sha>' via 'git rev-parse --short HEAD'

The method signature stays fn git_branch(&self) -> Option<String>. Only the body changes.

FILES: coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs (git_branch method, lines 394-402)

ACCEPTANCE: cargo test -p coordination passes. Add a unit test that mocks or simulates detached HEAD and verifies the fallback chain. cargo fmt --all -- --check and cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean.","notes":"SWARM-READY: 5-10 line change in a single method. {"id":"beefcake-swarm-xv9","title":"Handle detached HEAD in WorkPacketGenerator","description":"WorkPacketGenerator::git_branch() uses 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD' which returns the literal string 'HEAD' when in detached HEAD state (common in CI, after checkout of a specific commit, or in freshly created worktrees before the first commit).

This means the WorkPacket.branch field becomes 'HEAD' which is misleading in the prompt sent to the LLM and may confuse the agent about which branch it's working on.

Fix: If git_branch() returns 'HEAD', fall back to:
1. Check for CI env vars (CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, GITHUB_HEAD_REF, BRANCH_NAME)
2. Try 'git name-rev --name-only HEAD'
3. Use 'detached@<short-sha>' as a last resort

{"description":"ContextPacker::trim_to_budget() in coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs (line 348) drops file_contexts from the end via pop(). The ordering guarantee is implicit and fragile.

FIX (2 parts):

PART 1 - Add priority field to FileContext in coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs:
Add a 'priority: u8' field to the FileContext struct (after 'relevance'). Use these values:
- 0 = Error context (highest, never trim first)
- 1 = Modified file content
- 2 = File header / structural context
- 3 = Reference context (lowest, trim first)
Default to 2 if not specified. Add #[serde(default = "default_priority")] with fn default_priority() -> u8 { 2 }.

PART 2 - Sort before trimming in trim_to_budget() (line 348-354):
Before the while loop, sort file_contexts by priority descending (highest priority number last) so pop() removes lowest-priority contexts first:
  packet.file_contexts.sort_by(|a, b| a.priority.cmp(&b.priority));

PART 3 - Update call sites in generator.rs and packer.rs:
Set priority field when constructing FileContext instances:
- In generator.rs build_error_contexts(): priority = 0
- In generator.rs build_file_contexts() for modified files: priority = 1  
- In generator.rs build_file_contexts() for headers: priority = 2
- In packer.rs pack_initial() for worktree files: priority = 2

FILES: coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs, coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs, coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs

old_value:
{"id":"beefcake-swarm-ost","title":"Priority-scored context trimming in ContextPacker","description":"ContextPacker::trim_to_budget() drops file_contexts from the end via pop(). This happens to work correctly because WorkPacketGenerator inserts error contexts first (highest priority) and file header contexts last (lowest priority). But this ordering guarantee is implicit and fragile — any change to insertion order in a different module breaks trimming priority silently.

Fix: Add a priority field to FileContext (or use an enum: Error > Modified > Header > Reference). Before trimming, sort file_contexts by priority (lowest priority last). Then pop() is guaranteed to drop the least important context first regardless of insertion order.

Alternative: Instead of modifying FileContext, partition into priority buckets and trim from the lowest bucket first.

Files: coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs, coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-mu1","title":"Implement graceful shutdown with worktree cleanup","description":"The orchestrator has no signal handler. If killed via SIGINT (Ctrl+C) or SIGTERM (deployment, systemd stop) while a worktree is active, the worktree remains on disk and registered in git, causing disk space leaks and 'already checked out' errors on restart.

Fix: Use tokio::signal::ctrl_c() and tokio::signal::unix::signal(SignalKind::terminate()) to catch shutdown signals. When received:
1. Set a shutdown flag (AtomicBool or tokio::sync::watch)
2. Check the flag at the top of each loop iteration
3. On shutdown: log the in-progress issue ID, call worktree_bridge.remove_worktree(), update beads status back to 'open', then exit cleanly

Consider wrapping the active worktree path in an Arc<Mutex<Option<String>>> so the signal handler knows what to clean up. Requires the remove_worktree() method from the worktree cleanup issue.

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-ez1","title":"Add tracing spans with #[instrument] to key methods","description":"Current logging is flat — all log lines are at the same level with no hierarchical grouping. It's hard to correlate which logs belong to which iteration, issue, or git operation when reviewing output.

Fix: Add #[tracing::instrument] attributes to key methods:
- ContextPacker::pack_initial(bead_id, objective) — span includes bead_id
- ContextPacker::pack_retry(bead_id, ...) — span includes bead_id and iteration
- WorktreeBridge::create(issue_id) — span includes issue_id
- WorktreeBridge::merge_and_remove(issue_id) — span includes issue_id
- Verifier::run_pipeline() — span includes working_dir
- Implementer::implement() — span includes model name

Add skip directives for large parameters (e.g. task_description content). This creates nested spans visible in structured log output (JSON) or tracing-subscriber's hierarchical formatter.

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-6a1","title":"Add scope constraints to prevent workers from modifying existing code beyond task scope","description":"Job 1621: Worker was asked to add cleanup_worktree() method but also:
- Changed .status.success() to .status().success() in EVERY existing method (wrong API)
- Added .expect() after .context()? everywhere (nonsensical)
- Introduced a typo (from_utf8_lossii)
- Broke closing braces
- Deleted comments

The worker went far beyond scope — it touched every method in the file when it only needed to add one new method and wire it into one spot in orchestrator.rs.

FIX OPTIONS:
1. Add file-diff size limits: if worker modifies >N lines outside the target area, reject the commit
2. Add explicit scope constraints in the work packet: "ONLY modify these functions/methods, do NOT touch existing code"
3. Add a pre-commit check that compares the diff against the task description and rejects over-broad changes
4. Use git diff --stat to detect when changes are disproportionate to the task

Most practical: Option 2 — add scope constraints to the work packet prompt that explicitly tell the worker what files/methods it may modify and what it must not touch.","notes":"Partially mitigated by edit_file tool (beefcake-swarm-xom). {"id":"beefcake-873","title":"Implement structured session summaries (anchored iterative)","description":"Phase 1 Foundation. Context handoffs between tiers currently use freeform strings in WorkPacket. Replace with structured session summaries using anchored iterative compression: each iteration produces a structured delta (what changed, what error, what was tried), and the summary is built by folding deltas into a running structured summary.

Key files: coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs, context_packer/packer.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs
Crates: coordination + swarm-agents

Acceptance criteria:
- IterationDelta struct: files_touched, error_summary, approach_taken, outcome
- SessionSummary struct: task_description, iteration_deltas (Vec), running_summary, key_decisions
- ContextPacker::pack_session() produces SessionSummary from iteration history
- WorkPacket.context field accepts SessionSummary (backward compatible with string)
- Orchestrator populates IterationDelta at each iteration boundary
- Unit tests: delta folding, summary construction, round-trip serialization","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:17Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:17Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-lg0","title":"Add failsafe for NotebookLM query failures in orchestrator","description":"The orchestrator queries NotebookLM (project_brain, debugging_kb) before each iteration for context enrichment. When nlm query fails (e.g., auth expired, network timeout, nlm binary missing), it should degrade gracefully rather than propagating the error. Current behavior: the error is caught but the enrichment silently fails with an empty context addition. Need: explicit warning log, retry with exponential backoff (1 attempt), and a circuit breaker that disables NLM queries for the rest of the session after N consecutive failures. Also add a SWARM_NLM_ENABLED=0 env var to disable NLM queries entirely for testing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T22:14:30Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T22:14:30Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-e5b","title":"Implement friction detection signals","description":"Phase 2 Signal Detection. Detect behavioral friction — sessions where the agent is stuck but error counts alone don't reveal it. Friction signals: file churn (modifying same file 3+ times), error cycling (same error category recurring after supposed fix), stagnant error count (errors not decreasing across iterations), context overflow (work packet exceeds size threshold).

Depends on: issue 4 (artifact tracking for file churn data)
Key files: NEW coordination/src/analytics/friction.rs, coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs
Crates: coordination + swarm-agents

Acceptance criteria:
- FrictionSignal enum: FileChurn(path, count), ErrorCycling(category, occurrences), StagnantErrors(count, iterations), ContextOverflow(size_bytes)
- FrictionDetector with detect(session: &SessionTelemetry) -> Vec<FrictionSignal>
- Configurable thresholds per signal type
- Integration: orchestrator checks friction mid-session (not just at end) to enable early escalation
- Unit tests: synthetic sessions triggering each friction type","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:18Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:18Z"}

Depends on: issue 1 (telemetry reader for baseline averages)
Key files: NEW coordination/src/analytics/delight.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/telemetry.rs
Crates: coordination + swarm-agents

- DelightSignal enum: FirstTrySuccess, BelowAverageIterations, CleanDiff, NoEscalation, FastResolution
- DelightDetector struct with detect(session: &SessionTelemetry, baselines: &AggregateAnalytics) -> Vec<DelightSignal>
- Configurable thresholds (e.g., below_average_factor: 0.7)
- Integration point: orchestrator calls detect() at session end, stores signals in telemetry

{"id":"beefcake-6b4","title":"Implement dynamic coder routing from historical performance","description":"Phase 2 Signal Detection. Currently the router uses static rules (type mismatch → strand, borrow errors → OR1). Replace with dynamic routing that uses historical per-agent performance data: if strand-14B has 90% success on borrow errors for small files, route there instead of escalating to OR1.

Depends on: issues 1 (telemetry reader), 2 (per-agent tracking)
Key files: NEW coordination/src/analytics/routing.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs
Crates: coordination + swarm-agents

Acceptance criteria:
- RoutingScore struct: model_id, task_type, historical_success_rate, mean_iterations, confidence (sample_count)
- DynamicRouter that queries AggregateAnalytics to compute RoutingScores
- Fallback to static routing when confidence is below threshold (cold-start)
- Router selection: highest success_rate among models with confidence >= threshold, breaking ties by mean_iterations
- Integration: orchestrator uses DynamicRouter when available, falls back to static Router
- Unit tests: routing with sufficient/insufficient data, tie-breaking, cold-start fallback","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:19Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:19Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-7kj","title":"Implement structured escalation heuristics from telemetry","description":"Phase 3 Skill Library. Current escalation triggers are hardcoded (error repeat 2x, >3 compile failures, >8 files). Replace with telemetry-calibrated thresholds: compute per-model, per-task-type escalation points from historical data. If strand-14B typically solves borrow errors in 2 iterations, escalate at 3 (not the global default of 2).

Depends on: issues 1 (telemetry reader), 8 (dynamic routing)
Key files: NEW coordination/src/analytics/calibration.rs, coordination/src/escalation/engine.rs
Crate: coordination

Acceptance criteria:
- EscalationThreshold struct: model_id, task_type, max_iterations, max_same_error, max_files_changed
- ThresholdCalibrator: calibrate(analytics: &AggregateAnalytics) -> Vec<EscalationThreshold>
- Calibration formula: p75 of historical iterations for successful sessions (with minimum sample count)
- EscalationEngine accepts dynamic thresholds, falls back to static defaults when uncalibrated
- Integration: engine loads calibrated thresholds at startup, refreshes periodically
- Unit tests: calibration from synthetic data, fallback behavior, threshold application","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:20Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:20Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-a3y","title":"Improve token counting accuracy (bytes vs chars)","description":"Fix token counting in coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs.

WorkPacket::estimated_tokens() uses json.len() / 4 where String::len() returns byte count not character count. Multi-byte UTF-8 characters cause overestimation.

FIX in estimated_tokens() method:
1. Change json.len() / 4 to json.chars().count() / 4
2. Apply a safety margin: multiply result by 1.1 (10% buffer) to avoid context overflow

ACCEPTANCE: cargo test -p coordination passes. Add a test with a WorkPacket containing unicode strings and verify estimated_tokens() returns reasonable values. cargo fmt and cargo clippy clean.","notes":"SWARM-READY: Change json.len()/4 to json.chars().count()/4 plus add a test. Single-line fix.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:13:31Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-17T09:13:42Z","labels":["context","swarm-ready"]}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-ost","title":"Priority-scored context trimming in ContextPacker","description":"ContextPacker::trim_to_budget() in coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs (line 348) drops file_contexts from the end via pop(). The ordering guarantee is implicit and fragile.

FIX (2 parts):

PART 1 - Add priority field to FileContext in coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs:
Add a 'priority: u8' field to the FileContext struct (after 'relevance'). Use these values:
- 0 = Error context (highest, never trim first)
- 1 = Modified file content
- 2 = File header / structural context
- 3 = Reference context (lowest, trim first)
Default to 2 if not specified. Add #[serde(default = "default_priority")] with fn default_priority() -> u8 { 2 }.

PART 2 - Sort before trimming in trim_to_budget() (line 348-354):
Before the while loop, sort file_contexts by priority descending (highest priority number last) so pop() removes lowest-priority contexts first:
  packet.file_contexts.sort_by(|a, b| a.priority.cmp(&b.priority));

PART 3 - Update call sites in generator.rs and packer.rs:
Set priority field when constructing FileContext instances:
- In generator.rs build_error_contexts(): priority = 0
- In generator.rs build_file_contexts() for modified files: priority = 1  
- In generator.rs build_file_contexts() for headers: priority = 2
- In packer.rs pack_initial() for worktree files: priority = 2

FILES: coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs, coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs, coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs

ACCEPTANCE: cargo test -p coordination passes. Add a test that verifies trim_to_budget drops low-priority contexts before high-priority ones. cargo fmt and cargo clippy clean.","notes":"SWARM-READY: Add one field to struct, one sort_by call, set priority values at ~4 construction sites. All in coordination crate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:13:33Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-18T21:55:54Z","labels":["context","swarm-ready"]}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-4p5","title":"Iteration delta memory (structured context evolution)","description":"UNIQUE INSIGHT from GPT-5.2-Codex and Opus

Replace flat 'previous_attempts' strings with structured iteration deltas that capture WHAT CHANGED between iterations, not just what happened.

Add IterationDelta struct:
- fixed_errors: Vec<ErrorCategory> — what improved
- new_errors: Vec<ErrorCategory> — what regressed  
- files_modified: Vec<String> — what was touched
- hypothesis: Option<String> — what the model claimed it was doing (extracted from response)
- result_summary: String — 'borrow error fixed, but introduced lifetime error in return type'
- strategy_used: String — 'added Arc wrapper' or 'changed lifetime annotation'

Include only the last 2-3 deltas in context (not full history). The model needs to see 'you fixed X but broke Y' not 'here is everything that ever went wrong'.

Also add provenance tags to FileContext (source: compiler_error, diff, dependency, import) and decay: reduce weight of previously included context not re-referenced by new errors.

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-2jk","title":"Extract shared SourceFileProvider to deduplicate file reading","description":"Both ContextPacker::build_file_contexts() and WorkPacketGenerator::extract_symbols_from_files() independently read files from disk and iterate lines. This duplicates I/O when both are called in sequence (e.g. pack_initial calls the generator then builds its own contexts).

Fix: Extract a SourceFileProvider struct that:
1. Caches file content in a HashMap<PathBuf, String> on first read
2. Provides methods: get_content(path) -> &str, get_header(path, lines: usize) -> &str, get_lines_around(path, line, context) -> &str
3. Is shared between WorkPacketGenerator and ContextPacker via reference

This eliminates redundant disk reads and provides a single point for file access patterns.

Files: coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs, coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs

This duplicates I/O when both are called in sequence (e.g. pack_initial calls the generator then builds its own contexts).

Fix: Extract a SourceFileProvider struct that:
1. Caches file content in a HashMap<PathBuf, String> on first read
2. Provides methods: get_content(path) -> &str, get_header(path, lines: usize) -> &str, get_lines_around(path, line, context) -> &str
3. Is shared between WorkPacketGenerator and ContextPacker via reference

This eliminates redundant disk reads and provides a single point for file access patterns.

Files: coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs, coordination/src/context_packer/packer.rs

Build a persistent skill library that indexes successful patterns from retrospectives. Skills are keyed by trigger context (error category + file pattern + task type) and store the approach that worked. When a new task matches a skill's trigger, the skill is injected into the work packet as a hint.

Depends on: issues 1 (telemetry), 2 (per-agent tracking), 7 (retrospective), 9 (knowledge capture)
Key files: NEW coordination/src/analytics/skills.rs, coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs
Crates: coordination + swarm-agents

- Skill struct: id, trigger (SkillTrigger), approach (String), success_count, failure_count, confidence
- SkillTrigger struct: error_categories (Vec), file_patterns (Vec<glob>), task_type (Option)
- SkillLibrary: add_skill(), find_matching(context: &TaskContext) -> Vec<Skill> (ranked by confidence)
- Confidence formula: success_count / (success_count + failure_count) with minimum sample threshold
- RocksDB persistence (reuse existing state infrastructure)
- WorkPacket gains skill_hints: Vec<SkillHint> field
- Orchestrator queries library before dispatching, injects matching skills
- Unit tests: skill matching, confidence scoring, persistence round-trip","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:19Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:19Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-90u","title":"Implement conservative acceptance for self-modifications","description":"Phase 4 Safety. The skill library and calibrated thresholds are forms of self-modification — the swarm changes its own behavior based on experience. Implement conservative acceptance: new skills/thresholds must pass a validation period before being promoted to active use. Prevents a single lucky session from permanently biasing routing or escalation.

Depends on: issues 10 (skill library), 12 (escalation heuristics)
Key files: NEW coordination/src/analytics/verification.rs, coordination/src/analytics/skills.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs
Crates: coordination + swarm-agents

Acceptance criteria:
- AcceptanceStatus enum: Candidate, Probation(uses_remaining), Accepted, Rejected
- AcceptancePolicy: min_uses_before_promotion (default: 5), min_success_rate (default: 0.7), probation_period (default: 10 uses)
- VerificationTracker: track_usage(skill_id, outcome) -> AcceptanceStatus transition
- Skills start as Candidate, move to Probation after first success, promoted to Accepted after meeting policy thresholds
- Rejected skills are soft-deleted (kept for analysis but not matched)
- Escalation thresholds follow same acceptance pipeline
- Orchestrator only uses Accepted skills/thresholds by default, Probation ones marked as experimental
- Unit tests: full lifecycle Candidate → Probation → Accepted, Candidate → Probation → Rejected","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:21Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:21Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-6df","title":"Implement experience replay for successful traces","description":"Phase 3 Skill Library. When a new task resembles a previously successful one, replay the successful trace as a structured hint. Experience replay provides the full sequence of actions (not just the final pattern like skills) — useful for multi-step fixes where order matters.

Depends on: issues 3 (structured summaries), 4 (artifact tracking), 10 (skill library)
Key files: NEW coordination/src/analytics/replay.rs, coordination/src/work_packet/types.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs
Crates: coordination + swarm-agents

Acceptance criteria:
- ExperienceTrace struct: task_context, iteration_sequence (Vec<IterationDelta>), outcome, artifacts, total_duration
- TraceIndex: index by task_type + error_categories + file_patterns (similar to skill triggers)
- ExperienceReplay: find_similar(context: &TaskContext, k: usize) -> Vec<ExperienceTrace>
- Similarity scoring: weighted overlap of error categories, file patterns, task type
- WorkPacket gains replay_hints: Vec<ReplayHint> (condensed trace summaries)
- Orchestrator queries replay before dispatch, injects top-k hints
- RocksDB persistence for trace storage
- Unit tests: indexing, similarity scoring, retrieval, hint injection","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:20Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:17:20Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-xv9","title":"Handle detached HEAD in WorkPacketGenerator","description":"WorkPacketGenerator::git_branch() in coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs (line 394) uses 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD' which returns the literal string 'HEAD' when in detached HEAD state (common in CI, after checkout of a specific commit, or in freshly created worktrees before the first commit).

This means the WorkPacket.branch field becomes 'HEAD' which is misleading in the prompt sent to the LLM.

FIX in git_branch() method (line 394-402):
1. If result == 'HEAD', check CI env vars: CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, GITHUB_HEAD_REF, BRANCH_NAME
2. If still 'HEAD', try 'git name-rev --name-only HEAD' and strip remotes/ prefix
3. If still 'HEAD', compute 'detached@<short-sha>' via 'git rev-parse --short HEAD'

The method signature stays fn git_branch(&self) -> Option<String>. Only the body changes.

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-6a1","title":"Add scope constraints to prevent workers from modifying existing code beyond task scope","description":"Job 1621: Worker was asked to add cleanup_worktree() method but also:
- Changed .status.success() to .status().success() in EVERY existing method (wrong API)
- Added .expect() after .context()? everywhere (nonsensical)
- Introduced a typo (from_utf8_lossii)
- Broke closing braces
- Deleted comments

The worker went far beyond scope — it touched every method in the file when it only needed to add one new method and wire it into one spot in orchestrator.rs.

FIX OPTIONS:
1. Add file-diff size limits: if worker modifies >N lines outside the target area, reject the commit
2. Add explicit scope constraints in the work packet: "ONLY modify these functions/methods, do NOT touch existing code"
3. Add a pre-commit check that compares the diff against the task description and rejects over-broad changes
4. Use git diff --stat to detect when changes are disproportionate to the task

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-7go","title":"Detect and clean zombie branches on startup","description":"If the orchestrator crashes mid-loop, /tmp may clean the worktree directory but the swarm/<issue_id> branch persists in the repo. On next run, git worktree add -b fails with 'A branch named swarm/... already exists' and the agent gets permanently stuck on that issue.

Fix: On startup (before the main loop), scan for orphaned swarm/* branches that have no corresponding worktree directory. For each orphan: attempt git branch -D to delete it. Also check git worktree list --porcelain for stale entries and run git worktree prune. This requires the remove_worktree() method from the worktree cleanup issue.

Add a WorktreeBridge::cleanup_stale() method that:
1. Runs git worktree prune
2. Lists all branches matching swarm/*
3. For each, checks if worktree_path(id) exists
4. If not, force-deletes the branch

Call cleanup_stale() in main() before entering the issue-picking loop.

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-dxt","title":"Atomic issue claiming to prevent race conditions","description":"The orchestrator has a check-then-act race: it calls list_open(), sorts by priority, picks the first issue, then calls update_status(in_progress). If two orchestrator instances run simultaneously (or two swarm loops), both will pick the same P1 issue, leading to duplicate work, git conflicts, and wasted inference credits.

Fix: Either implement an atomic claim_next_available() in BeadsBridge that combines list+claim in a single operation, or handle the case where update_status returns 'already claimed' and retry with the next issue. Consider adding a locking mechanism (file lock or beads-level CAS).

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-j4l","title":"Add Planner agent with structured change contracts","description":"UNANIMOUS CONSENSUS (4/4 models: G3-Pro, GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.2, Opus 4.5)

The current loop goes straight to code generation without formalizing intent. Add a Planner agent (can use the fast 14B model) that runs BEFORE the Implementer and produces a structured 'change contract':

- Acceptance criteria (what must be true when done)
- Invariants / behavioral promises (what must NOT change)
- Files expected to change (scoping)
- Risk classification (API break risk, concurrency risk, security risk)
- Test plan: which tests to add/modify, what failure proves correctness

The contract becomes:
1. A constraint for the Implementer (bounded search space)
2. A checklist for the Validator (check against plan, not just 'does it look right')
3. An audit trail (what was intended vs what happened)
4. Partial rollback points (if step 3/5 fails, retry from step 3)

Implementation: Add a PlannerAgent trait and struct in swarm-agents, produce a ChangeContract type in coordination/. The contract feeds into WorkPacket.constraints and WorkPacket.decisions. The Validator receives the contract alongside the diff.

G3-Pro calls this 'Legislator-Executor'. GPT-5.2-Codex calls it '3-stage contract + selection'. Both identify it as the highest-impact architectural change.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/ (new planner module), coordination/src/ (new contract types), crates/swarm-agents/src/main.rs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:34:21Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-18T21:20:46Z","labels":["architecture"]}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-ty0","title":"Use structured error fields in tracing macros","description":"Error logging uses string interpolation: error!('Failed to create worktree: {e}'). This embeds the error message in the format string, making it impossible for log aggregators (Loki, Datadog) to parse the error type separately from the message.

Fix: Use structured fields in tracing macros throughout main.rs and worktree_bridge.rs:
- error!(error = %e, issue_id = %id, 'Failed to create worktree') — Display format
- error!(error = ?e, issue_id = %id, 'Failed to create worktree') — Debug format (more detail)
- warn!(iteration, feedback = %result.feedback, 'Validator FAILED')

This allows querying logs by error type, issue_id, or iteration number independently.

old_value:
{"id":"beefcake-swarm-jse","title":"Sandbox verification pipeline (treat agent code as untrusted)","description":"STRONG CONSENSUS (3/4 models)

Agent-generated code can do ANYTHING — including filesystem deletion, network exfiltration, command execution in build.rs, or infinite loops in tests. On an HPC cluster with shared NFS, this is catastrophic.

Run all verification gates in a sandbox:
1. Use bubblewrap (bwrap) or firejail on Linux to sandbox cargo commands
2. Restrictions: no network access, restricted filesystem (only worktree + target dirs), CPU/memory/time caps
3. Per-test timeouts (not just per-gate — the existing gate_timeout_secs config isn't enforced)
4. Kill process trees on timeout (not just the parent process)
5. Detect 'test passed but took 10x longer than baseline' as suspicious

Also add: pre-check for dangerous patterns before running (new unsafe blocks, std::process::Command, std::fs::remove_dir_all, network calls).

Note: VerifierConfig already has gate_timeout_secs (line 28 in pipeline.rs) but it's not enforced — gates use blocking Command::output() with no timeout.

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-pid","title":"Expand verifier with Miri, proptest, cargo-deny gates","description":"STRONG CONSENSUS (3/4 models)

The verifier only runs fmt/clippy/check/test. Add risk-based verification gates:

High-value additions:
1. cargo miri test — UB detection for unsafe-heavy code (mandatory for any unsafe blocks)
2. cargo deny check — security advisories, banned crates, license compliance
3. cargo semver-checks — prevent accidental breaking API changes
4. cargo udeps — detect unused dependencies introduced by agents
5. nextest — faster test execution with better isolation + flaky detection
6. Feature matrix: test with --no-default-features and key feature flags
7. Doc tests / rustdoc lints for public API changes

Make gates ADAPTIVE: select which extra gates to run based on diff risk profile:
- Touches unsafe → require Miri
- Changes Cargo.toml → require deny + udeps
- Changes public API → require semver-checks
- All changes → nextest with flaky detection

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-5uq","title":"Add Adversarial Breaker agent for pre-merge red-teaming","description":"STRONG CONSENSUS (3/4 models)

Before merging, a dedicated agent should actively try to BREAK the implementation by generating edge-case tests, weird inputs, concurrency interleavings, and invalid states.

The SwarmTier::Adversary already exists in escalation/state.rs but isn't integrated into the loop.

Implementation:
1. After Verifier passes (all green), invoke the Adversary agent
2. Give it ONLY the diff and public API signatures (no implementation context — truly adversarial)
3. It generates: proptest harnesses, edge-case unit tests, boundary condition checks
4. Run these generated tests through the Verifier
5. If any Adversary test fails, reject the implementation and feed the failing test back to the Implementer

This catches 'compiles but is wrong' — the biggest failure mode that compilation gates miss.

G3-Pro: 'Active Sabotage / Mutation Testing'. Opus: 'Adversary actively tries to break the code'.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/ (new adversary module), crates/swarm-agents/src/main.rs","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:34:44Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-16T13:03:00Z","labels":["architecture"]}

The SwarmTier::Adversary already exists in escalation/state.rs but isn't integrated into the loop.

Implementation:
1. After Verifier passes (all green), invoke the Adversary agent
2. Give it ONLY the diff and public API signatures (no implementation context — truly adversarial)
3. It generates: proptest harnesses, edge-case unit tests, boundary condition checks
4. Run these generated tests through the Verifier
5. If any Adversary test fails, reject the implementation and feed the failing test back to the Implementer

This catches 'compiles but is wrong' — the biggest failure mode that compilation gates miss.

G3-Pro: 'Active Sabotage / Mutation Testing'. Opus: 'Adversary actively tries to break the code'.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/ (new adversary module), crates/swarm-agents/src/main.rs","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:34:44Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-02-16T13:03:00Z","labels":["architecture"]}

{"id":"beefcake-swarm-zg6","title":"Multi-proposal speculative execution for hard tasks","description":"UNANIMOUS CONSENSUS (4/4 models)

For hard/risky tasks, a single implementer often gets stuck in local minima. All 4 models propose spawning multiple implementations in parallel.

Design:
1. Classify issue difficulty (simple vs hard) based on: error category history, file count, risk classification from Planner
2. For hard tasks, spawn 2-3 Implementers in parallel with different strategies:
   - Different temperature settings (0.2 conservative, 0.8 creative)
   - Different system prompts ('minimal fix' vs 'refactor for clarity')
   - Different model tiers (14B fast attempt + 72B deep attempt)
3. Each produces a patch in its own worktree
4. Run Verifier on all patches in parallel (CPU-bound, manageable)
5. If multiple pass, Validator ranks and picks the best (most concise, least risk)
6. If none pass, combine insights from all attempts for the retry context

This trades compute for success rate. G3-Pro: 'Parallel Speculative Decoding'. GPT-5.2: 'Selection via competition — turn merges into a market'.

{"id":"beefcake-676t","title":"Swarm: no cloud endpoint health check in cloud-only mode before entering loop","description":"In cloud-only mode, the orchestrator skips local endpoint health checks (as expected) but also doesn't verify the cloud endpoint is reachable. It proceeds directly to worktree creation and the first LLM prompt, wasting ~80s on HTTP timeout before discovering the endpoint is down. Fix: add a lightweight HEAD or GET /v1/models check against cloud_endpoint.url before creating the worktree. Should fail fast with a clear error message. File: crates/swarm-agents/src/main.rs (health check section, ~line 83) and/or crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs (pre-loop). Discovered during swarm validation run.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T14:21:54Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-23T14:21:54Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-6pho","title":"Swarm: silent verifier run after agent failure creates apparent hang","description":"After an agent failure (e.g., HTTP error), the orchestrator runs the full verifier pipeline (fmt+clippy+check+test) but emits no log before starting it. The verifier can take 30-120s for a full workspace, during which the process appears hung — no output to stdout/stderr. Fix: add an info\\! log line before the verifier.run_pipeline() call in the Err(e) branch (orchestrator.rs ~line 1236), e.g., 'Running verifier after agent failure (iteration N)'. File: crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs. Discovered during swarm validation run — process appeared hung for minutes after the HTTP error.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T14:22:03Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-23T14:22:03Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-noqp","title":"Unblock Qwen3.5-397B chat completions for dogfooding","description":"Apply Codex-recommended debugging steps to fix the Qwen3.5-397B chat completions issue where the model produces flat/random logits after chat template special tokens.

Symptom: /v1/completions works perfectly (sharp logits, coherent output). /v1/chat/completions produces garbage or immediate EOS after processing <|im_start|>, <|im_end|>, <think> tokens. CPU-only reproduces it, ruling out GPU/offload issues.

Codex analysis (2026-02-25): Points to chat-path/template-token/tokenizer-metadata interaction, NOT GPU offloading or quantization.

Debugging steps (priority order):
1. A/B test: render prompt with /apply-template, feed exact string to /v1/completions
   - If works: bug is in chat completions server path (message assembly/prefill/reasoning)
   - If fails: bug is in template tokens / tokenizer metadata / GGUF
2. Try all chat server flags together: --reasoning-format none --reasoning-budget 0 --no-prefill-assistant --no-cache-prompt --cache-reuse 0
3. Compare /tokenize output for <|im_start|> (248045), <|im_end|> (248046), <think> against upstream HF tokenizer for Qwen3.5-MoE — check for GGUF tokenizer metadata mismatch
4. Upgrade llama.cpp past b8145:
   - PR #19849 (merged 2026-02-25): hybrid/recurrent server checkpointing fix (n_past vs position)
   - PR #19866 (merged 2026-02-24): Qwen 3.5 multi-GPU graph split ordering
   - PR #19877 (merged 2026-02-25): re-enable multimodal prompt caching
5. Try a different GGUF source for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (different conversion pipeline)
6. If all else fails: plain-text instruct scaffold avoiding special tokens entirely (System:/User:/Assistant: format via completions endpoint)

Current state:
- vasp-01: Job 1807 running with OLD config (no --jinja, proxy on :8181)
- vasp-02: No SLURM job running
- Chat proxy deployed at /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Best result so far: 57 tokens with --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking": false}' (garbled quality)
- Tracked upstream: llama.cpp #19690, #19858

Acceptance: Qwen3.5-397B responds coherently to chat completions requests with >200 token outputs and sharp logits, OR a reliable workaround is documented and integrated into the proxy.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:30Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:30Z"}

{"notes":"## Session 3 Findings (2026-02-25)

### UD-Q4_K_XL is fundamentally broken
Comprehensive testing on BOTH nodes (vasp-01 and vasp-02) confirms:
- Even simple text continuation ("The history of computing...") now degenerates
- Code completion works only for trivial 9-token outputs (hello world)
- Longer code completion produces deeply nested parentheses (garbage)
- --jinja vs no --jinja makes no difference — model itself is broken
- Model files identical on both nodes (same timestamps, same sizes)
- This is NOT a proxy issue — the raw /v1/completions endpoint produces garbage

### Testing results (all prompts via /v1/completions, no proxy)
| Prompt | Node | --jinja | Tokens | Quality |
|--------|------|---------|--------|---------|
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-02 | on | 128 | "A mechanical calculator" repeated |
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-01 | off | 35 | Partial coherence then repetition |
| `fn main() { println!("Hello` | vasp-01 | off | 9 | Correct (", world!");\n}) |
| is_prime function body | vasp-01 | off | 256 | Deeply nested parentheses |

### Root cause
Unsloth Dynamic 2.0 Q4_K_XL quantization corrupts model weights for this hybrid
Mamba/attention architecture. The dynamic precision upcasting doesn't preserve the
SSM (Mamba) layer computations, causing logit instability that worsens with output length.

### Recovery plan
1. lmstudio-community Q4_K_M download in progress on slurm-ctl (NFS)
   - Standard llama.cpp b8086 quantization (no Unsloth dynamic precision)
   - 241GB, 7 shards, downloading to /cluster/shared/ai/models/lmstudio-Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-GGUF/
   - ETA: ~4 hours from 23:35 EST (02:30-03:30 AM EST)
2. Test Q4_K_M directly from NFS path (no need to copy to /scratch initially)
3. If Q4_K_M works: update MODEL path, may not even need the proxy
4. If Q4_K_M also fails: issue is in llama.cpp qwen35moe arch, not quantization

### Chat proxy v3 status
- Deployed to /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Uses pure text continuation (no special tokens)
- Missing _format_tool_definitions added
- Docstring updated from v2 to v3
- SLURM script updated: JINJA_MODE env var (default: on, set off to disable)
- start-proxy.sh launcher script added for manual restart"}

{"notes":"## Session 4 Findings (2026-02-26)

### ik_llama.cpp built and tested
Built ik_llama.cpp fork (v1, 216f443) on cluster with GCC 12.4.0 + CUDA 12.6.
Binary at /cluster/shared/llama-cpp/ik-bin/llama-server, wrapper at run-ik-server.sh
(uses conda glibc 2.34 to work on Rocky Linux 8.8).

### ik_llama.cpp --cpu-moe results (GPU attention, CPU MoE)
- Server starts fine, MoE tensors confirmed on CPU
- Throughput: 10.3 tok/s gen, 43.6 tok/s prompt — good speed\!
- Quality: DEGENERATE — same garbage as mainline GPU mode
- Code continuation (is_prime): repetitive "let mut result = true;" — 178 tokens of garbage
- Text continuation: "a for input and (B for input)" — garbled
- Conclusion: --cpu-moe does NOT fix UD-Q4_K_XL quality. GPU attention also broken.

### ik_llama.cpp CPU-only (-ngl 0, CUDA hidden) results
- Throughput: 4.5 tok/s gen, 30 tok/s prompt (faster than mainline CPU-only at 1.9 tok/s)
- Text continuation with ignore_eos (200 tokens): somewhat coherent (history of
  computing, punched cards, Hollerith) but has garbled words and repetitive themes
- Without ignore_eos: 6-10 tokens then EOS (coherent but too short)
- Proxy test (chat completions through proxy): degenerate garbage — "Task:" format
  still triggers degeneration even with pure text continuation

### Definitive conclusion: UD-Q4_K_XL quantization is fundamentally broken
Tested across ALL combinations:
| Backend | GPU Mode | Tok/s | Quality |
|---------|----------|-------|---------|
| Mainline | GPU (-ngl 99) | ~8.4 | Degenerate (issue #19683) |
| Mainline | CPU (-ngl 0, CUDA loaded) | 1.9 | 128 tokens coherent then CRASH |
| Mainline | CPU (-ngl 0, no CUDA) | 2.1 | 26 tokens mediocre |
| ik_llama.cpp | --cpu-moe (-ngl 99) | 10.3 | Degenerate |
| ik_llama.cpp | CPU (-ngl 0, no CUDA) | 4.5 | Mediocre/short |

NO configuration produces reliable instruction-following output with UD-Q4_K_XL.

### Infrastructure updates
- SLURM script: added LLAMA_BACKEND env var ("mainline" or "ik")
- SLURM script: ik_llama.cpp binary path, --cpu-moe flag, wrapper launch
- ik_llama.cpp ready on cluster for testing with different quantizations

### Path forward: lmstudio Q4_K_M
Download at 104GB/241GB (~43%) on slurm-ctl. Standard GGML quantization.
Concern: 241GB model might not fit in 256GB RAM with KV cache.

{"id":"beefcake-noqp","title":"Unblock Qwen3.5-397B chat completions for dogfooding","description":"Apply Codex-recommended debugging steps to fix the Qwen3.5-397B chat completions issue where the model produces flat/random logits after chat template special tokens.

Symptom: /v1/completions works perfectly (sharp logits, coherent output). /v1/chat/completions produces garbage or immediate EOS after processing <|im_start|>, <|im_end|>, <think> tokens. CPU-only reproduces it, ruling out GPU/offload issues.

Codex analysis (2026-02-25): Points to chat-path/template-token/tokenizer-metadata interaction, NOT GPU offloading or quantization.

Debugging steps (priority order):
1. A/B test: render prompt with /apply-template, feed exact string to /v1/completions
   - If works: bug is in chat completions server path (message assembly/prefill/reasoning)
   - If fails: bug is in template tokens / tokenizer metadata / GGUF
2. Try all chat server flags together: --reasoning-format none --reasoning-budget 0 --no-prefill-assistant --no-cache-prompt --cache-reuse 0
3. Compare /tokenize output for <|im_start|> (248045), <|im_end|> (248046), <think> against upstream HF tokenizer for Qwen3.5-MoE — check for GGUF tokenizer metadata mismatch
4. Upgrade llama.cpp past b8145:
   - PR #19849 (merged 2026-02-25): hybrid/recurrent server checkpointing fix (n_past vs position)
   - PR #19866 (merged 2026-02-24): Qwen 3.5 multi-GPU graph split ordering
   - PR #19877 (merged 2026-02-25): re-enable multimodal prompt caching
5. Try a different GGUF source for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (different conversion pipeline)
6. If all else fails: plain-text instruct scaffold avoiding special tokens entirely (System:/User:/Assistant: format via completions endpoint)

Current state:
- vasp-01: Job 1807 running with OLD config (no --jinja, proxy on :8181)
- vasp-02: No SLURM job running
- Chat proxy deployed at /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Best result so far: 57 tokens with --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking": false}' (garbled quality)
- Tracked upstream: llama.cpp #19690, #19858

Acceptance: Qwen3.5-397B responds coherently to chat completions requests with >200 token outputs and sharp logits, OR a reliable workaround is documented and integrated into the proxy.","notes":"## Session 3 Findings (2026-02-25)

### UD-Q4_K_XL is fundamentally broken
Comprehensive testing on BOTH nodes (vasp-01 and vasp-02) confirms:
- Even simple text continuation ("The history of computing...") now degenerates
- Code completion works only for trivial 9-token outputs (hello world)
- Longer code completion produces deeply nested parentheses (garbage)
- --jinja vs no --jinja makes no difference — model itself is broken
- Model files identical on both nodes (same timestamps, same sizes)
- This is NOT a proxy issue — the raw /v1/completions endpoint produces garbage

### Testing results (all prompts via /v1/completions, no proxy)
| Prompt | Node | --jinja | Tokens | Quality |
|--------|------|---------|--------|---------|
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-02 | on | 128 | "A mechanical calculator" repeated |
| "The history of computing..." | vasp-01 | off | 35 | Partial coherence then repetition |
| `fn main() { println!("Hello` | vasp-01 | off | 9 | Correct (", world!");\n}) |
| is_prime function body | vasp-01 | off | 256 | Deeply nested parentheses |

### Root cause
Unsloth Dynamic 2.0 Q4_K_XL quantization corrupts model weights for this hybrid
Mamba/attention architecture. The dynamic precision upcasting doesn't preserve the
SSM (Mamba) layer computations, causing logit instability that worsens with output length.

### Recovery plan
1. lmstudio-community Q4_K_M download in progress on slurm-ctl (NFS)
   - Standard llama.cpp b8086 quantization (no Unsloth dynamic precision)
   - 241GB, 7 shards, downloading to /cluster/shared/ai/models/lmstudio-Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-GGUF/
   - ETA: ~4 hours from 23:35 EST (02:30-03:30 AM EST)
2. Test Q4_K_M directly from NFS path (no need to copy to /scratch initially)
3. If Q4_K_M works: update MODEL path, may not even need the proxy
4. If Q4_K_M also fails: issue is in llama.cpp qwen35moe arch, not quantization

### Chat proxy v3 status
- Deployed to /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Uses pure text continuation (no special tokens)
- Missing _format_tool_definitions added
- Docstring updated from v2 to v3
- SLURM script updated: JINJA_MODE env var (default: on, set off to disable)
- start-proxy.sh launcher script added for manual restart","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:30Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T23:57:30Z"}

{"notes":"## Session 4 Findings (2026-02-26)

### ik_llama.cpp built and tested
Built ik_llama.cpp fork (v1, 216f443) on cluster with GCC 12.4.0 + CUDA 12.6.
Binary at /cluster/shared/llama-cpp/ik-bin/llama-server, wrapper at run-ik-server.sh
(uses conda glibc 2.34 to work on Rocky Linux 8.8).

### ik_llama.cpp --cpu-moe results (GPU attention, CPU MoE)
- Server starts fine, MoE tensors confirmed on CPU
- Throughput: 10.3 tok/s gen, 43.6 tok/s prompt — good speed\!
- Quality: DEGENERATE — same garbage as mainline GPU mode
- Code continuation (is_prime): repetitive "let mut result = true;" — 178 tokens of garbage
- Text continuation: "a for input and (B for input)" — garbled
- Conclusion: --cpu-moe does NOT fix UD-Q4_K_XL quality. GPU attention also broken.

### ik_llama.cpp CPU-only (-ngl 0, CUDA hidden) results
- Throughput: 4.5 tok/s gen, 30 tok/s prompt (faster than mainline CPU-only at 1.9 tok/s)
- Text continuation with ignore_eos (200 tokens): somewhat coherent (history of
  computing, punched cards, Hollerith) but has garbled words and repetitive themes
- Without ignore_eos: 6-10 tokens then EOS (coherent but too short)
- Proxy test (chat completions through proxy): degenerate garbage — "Task:" format
  still triggers degeneration even with pure text continuation

### Definitive conclusion: UD-Q4_K_XL quantization is fundamentally broken
Tested across ALL combinations:
| Backend | GPU Mode | Tok/s | Quality |
|---------|----------|-------|---------|
| Mainline | GPU (-ngl 99) | ~8.4 | Degenerate (issue #19683) |
| Mainline | CPU (-ngl 0, CUDA loaded) | 1.9 | 128 tokens coherent then CRASH |
| Mainline | CPU (-ngl 0, no CUDA) | 2.1 | 26 tokens mediocre |
| ik_llama.cpp | --cpu-moe (-ngl 99) | 10.3 | Degenerate |
| ik_llama.cpp | CPU (-ngl 0, no CUDA) | 4.5 | Mediocre/short |

NO configuration produces reliable instruction-following output with UD-Q4_K_XL.

### Infrastructure updates
- SLURM script: added LLAMA_BACKEND env var ("mainline" or "ik")
- SLURM script: ik_llama.cpp binary path, --cpu-moe flag, wrapper launch
- ik_llama.cpp ready on cluster for testing with different quantizations

### Path forward: lmstudio Q4_K_M
Download at 104GB/241GB (~43%) on slurm-ctl. Standard GGML quantization.
Concern: 241GB model might not fit in 256GB RAM with KV cache.
Will test with reduced slots when download completes.

### Cluster state
- vasp-01: idle (no SLURM job, /scratch 100% full)
- vasp-02: idle (CPU-only test server killed)
- slurm-ctl: Q4_K_M download running (PID 165874)"}

{"id":"beefcake-1s68","title":"Improve edit_file fuzzy matching for local model whitespace errors","description":"HydraCoder (30B MoE) generates edit_file calls where old_content doesn't exactly match the file text — typically whitespace differences (extra/missing spaces, indentation). The current whitespace-normalized fallback finds a match but often applies a no-op replacement because the new_content also has the same whitespace differences.

Two approaches to fix:
1. **Smarter fuzzy matching**: strip leading/trailing whitespace per line in both old_content and file text, then match. If found, apply new_content preserving the file's original indentation.
2. **Line-number-based edit mode**: add optional start_line/end_line params to edit_file so the model can specify a line range instead of exact text matching. This is more robust when the model has seen truncated file content.

Evidence from dogfooding: 3/6 iterations had edit_file calls that 'succeeded' via whitespace-normalized match but produced no actual file changes. The model understands what to change but can't produce exact text matches from truncated content.

{"id":"beefcake-1s68","title":"Improve edit_file fuzzy matching for local model whitespace errors","description":"HydraCoder (30B MoE) generates edit_file calls where old_content doesn't exactly match the file text — typically whitespace differences (extra/missing spaces, indentation). The current whitespace-normalized fallback finds a match but often applies a no-op replacement because the new_content also has the same whitespace differences.

Two approaches to fix:
1. **Smarter fuzzy matching**: strip leading/trailing whitespace per line in both old_content and file text, then match. If found, apply new_content preserving the file's original indentation.
2. **Line-number-based edit mode**: add optional start_line/end_line params to edit_file so the model can specify a line range instead of exact text matching. This is more robust when the model has seen truncated file content.

Evidence from dogfooding: 3/6 iterations had edit_file calls that 'succeeded' via whitespace-normalized match but produced no actual file changes. The model understands what to change but can't produce exact text matches from truncated content.

This is stochastic and prompt-content-dependent — the same prompt format sometimes triggers tool calls and sometimes doesn't.

Investigation areas:
1. **Temperature tuning**: test temp=0.05-0.1 to escape deterministic bad paths while maintaining reliability (current: 0.0)
2. **System prompt optimization**: the general_coder preamble (~1300 chars) may have content patterns that suppress tool calls. Try a minimal preamble.
3. **Retry on text-only response**: if the agent returns text without tool calls on turn 1, automatically retry the same prompt (stochastic behavior means retries may work)
4. **Qwen3.5-397B comparison**: the larger model may handle tool calling better — test when Q4_K_M download completes

Current reliability: 67% (4/6 iterations make tool calls). Target: >90%.

{"id":"beefcake-96l9","title":"Swarm: NotebookLM query fails silently with empty error message","description":"During swarm startup, the NotebookLM knowledge base query for 'project_brain' role fails with an empty error: 'KB query failed — proceeding without context role=project_brain error=nlm query failed: '. The error message after 'nlm query failed:' is empty, providing no diagnostic information. The nlm CLI likely isn't authenticated or isn't installed. Fix: (1) Log the full stderr/exit code from the nlm subprocess, not just the parsed error. (2) Consider checking nlm availability at startup and logging clearly if it's missing/unauthenticated. {"id":"beefcake-ft94","title":"Add line-range read_file variant for targeted file reading","description":"read_file currently returns the entire file (or truncated first N chars via SWARM_READ_FILE_MAX_CHARS). When a file is larger than the truncation limit (default 6000 chars), the model can only see the top portion and can't edit content later in the file.

Add optional start_line/end_line parameters to read_file so the model can request specific sections. This enables a workflow where the model:
1. Reads the file header/structure (default read)
2. Reads specific functions or sections by line range
3. Edits those sections with accurate old_content

This is especially important for local models with small context windows where full file content would suppress tool calls.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/tools/fs_tools.rs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T16:28:17Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T16:28:17Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-s0lh","title":"edit_file old_content drift after fuzzy-matched edits in same iteration","description":"When tool_choice=required forces multiple edit_file calls in one agent iteration, the model references its own previous edit text as old_content for subsequent edits. After the first fuzzy-matched edit rewrites the file (with reindentation), the model's cached version of the code no longer matches the actual file content. Both exact and fuzzy match fail on turns 4-5. Discovered in dogfood run 4. Possible fixes: (1) Include edit_file success feedback that shows the actual written content, (2) After a successful edit, force a read_file on the next turn, (3) Return the relevant portion of the new file content in the edit_file success response so the model has current state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:51Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:51Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-lsjm","title":"llama-server HTTP 500 from context overflow after many tool calls","description":"With tool_choice=required and max_turns=8, the accumulated context (system prompt + user message + multiple tool call/result pairs) can exceed llama-server's capacity after 6-7 turns. Observed in dogfood run 4, turn 7: HTTP 500 'Failed to parse input at pos 300'. Possible fixes: (1) Reduce DEFAULT_WORKER_MAX_TURNS to 5, (2) Add context-aware turn limit that tracks approximate token count, (3) Truncate tool results in agent history after each turn. Current: HydraCoder 30B-A3B MoE with 4 slots @ 32K context on vasp-02.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:52Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:52Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-vjuq","title":"Reduce stochastic tool-call failures in Worker tier prompts","description":"Despite compact prompts (~400 chars user message) and read_file truncation, ~33% of Worker tier iterations still produce text analysis instead of tool calls. This is stochastic and prompt-content-dependent — the same prompt format sometimes triggers tool calls and sometimes doesn't.

Investigation areas:
1. **Temperature tuning**: test temp=0.05-0.1 to escape deterministic bad paths while maintaining reliability (current: 0.0)
2. **System prompt optimization**: the general_coder preamble (~1300 chars) may have content patterns that suppress tool calls. Try a minimal preamble.
3. **Retry on text-only response**: if the agent returns text without tool calls on turn 1, automatically retry the same prompt (stochastic behavior means retries may work)
4. **Qwen3.5-397B comparison**: the larger model may handle tool calling better — test when Q4_K_M download completes

Current reliability: 67% (4/6 iterations make tool calls). Target: >90%.

Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs, crates/swarm-agents/src/prompts.rs","notes":"UPDATE 2026-02-26: tool_choice=required (commit 867343d) largely solves this. Run 4 showed 100% tool-call rate per turn (6/6 tool calls in iteration 2). The stochastic text-only failure mode is eliminated when the API forces tool calls. Remaining issues are now different: old_content drift after fuzzy edits, and context overflow after many turns. These are tracked in separate issues. Consider closing this issue or keeping it open to track edge cases where tool_choice=required doesn't work with certain models.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T16:28:18Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:53Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-lsjm","title":"llama-server HTTP 500 from context overflow after many tool calls","description":"With tool_choice=required and max_turns=8, the accumulated context (system prompt + user message + multiple tool call/result pairs) can exceed llama-server's capacity after 6-7 turns. Observed in dogfood run 4, turn 7: HTTP 500 'Failed to parse input at pos 300'. Possible fixes: (1) Reduce DEFAULT_WORKER_MAX_TURNS to 5, (2) Add context-aware turn limit that tracks approximate token count, (3) Truncate tool results in agent history after each turn. Current: HydraCoder 30B-A3B MoE with 4 slots @ 32K context on vasp-02.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:52Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T17:53:52Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-vjuq","title":"Reduce stochastic tool-call failures in Worker tier prompts","description":"Despite compact prompts (~400 chars user message) and read_file truncation, ~33% of Worker tier iterations still produce text analysis instead of tool calls. This is stochastic and prompt-content-dependent — the same prompt format sometimes triggers tool calls and sometimes doesn't.

Investigation areas:
1. **Temperature tuning**: test temp=0.05-0.1 to escape deterministic bad paths while maintaining reliability (current: 0.0)
2. **System prompt optimization**: the general_coder preamble (~1300 chars) may have content patterns that suppress tool calls. Try a minimal preamble.
3. **Retry on text-only response**: if the agent returns text without tool calls on turn 1, automatically retry the same prompt (stochastic behavior means retries may work)
4. **Qwen3.5-397B comparison**: the larger model may handle tool calling better — test when Q4_K_M download completes

Current reliability: 67% (4/6 iterations make tool calls). Target: >90%.

{"id":"beefcake-noqp","title":"Unblock Qwen3.5-397B chat completions for dogfooding","description":"Apply Codex-recommended debugging steps to fix the Qwen3.5-397B chat completions issue where the model produces flat/random logits after chat template special tokens.

Symptom: /v1/completions works perfectly (sharp logits, coherent output). /v1/chat/completions produces garbage or immediate EOS after processing <|im_start|>, <|im_end|>, <think> tokens. CPU-only reproduces it, ruling out GPU/offload issues.

Codex analysis (2026-02-25): Points to chat-path/template-token/tokenizer-metadata interaction, NOT GPU offloading or quantization.

Debugging steps (priority order):
1. A/B test: render prompt with /apply-template, feed exact string to /v1/completions
   - If works: bug is in chat completions server path (message assembly/prefill/reasoning)
   - If fails: bug is in template tokens / tokenizer metadata / GGUF
2. Try all chat server flags together: --reasoning-format none --reasoning-budget 0 --no-prefill-assistant --no-cache-prompt --cache-reuse 0
3. Compare /tokenize output for <|im_start|> (248045), <|im_end|> (248046), <think> against upstream HF tokenizer for Qwen3.5-MoE — check for GGUF tokenizer metadata mismatch
4. Upgrade llama.cpp past b8145:
   - PR #19849 (merged 2026-02-25): hybrid/recurrent server checkpointing fix (n_past vs position)
   - PR #19866 (merged 2026-02-24): Qwen 3.5 multi-GPU graph split ordering
   - PR #19877 (merged 2026-02-25): re-enable multimodal prompt caching
5. Try a different GGUF source for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (different conversion pipeline)
6. If all else fails: plain-text instruct scaffold avoiding special tokens entirely (System:/User:/Assistant: format via completions endpoint)

Current state:
- vasp-01: Job 1807 running with OLD config (no --jinja, proxy on :8181)
- vasp-02: No SLURM job running
- Chat proxy deployed at /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Best result so far: 57 tokens with --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking": false}' (garbled quality)
- Tracked upstream: llama.cpp #19690, #19858

{"id":"beefcake-wh8x","title":"Structured extraction via schemars::JsonSchema for typed LLM outputs","description":"Use Rig's structured extraction (agent.extract()) with schemars::JsonSchema derive to force LLMs to return typed Rust structs instead of parsing free-text responses.

Background: Currently the swarm parses LLM text output manually or uses basic JSON parsing. Rig's extract() method generates a JSON schema from Rust types and constrains the LLM to return conforming output, eliminating parsing failures.

Pattern from rig-examples:
```rust
#[derive(Deserialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
struct CodeReview {
    severity: String,
    issues: Vec<Issue>,
    suggestion: String,
    confidence: f64,
}

let review: CodeReview = agent.extract("Review this code: ...").await?;
```

Target types for structured extraction:
1. VerifierResult — pass/fail, error categories, fix suggestions
2. WorkPacketAnalysis — task classification, file contexts, key symbols, complexity score
3. CodeReviewOutput — severity, issues list, suggestions, confidence
4. EscalationDecision — should_escalate, reason, recommended_tier, context summary
5. PlannerOutput — steps list, file targets, dependency graph, risk assessment

Implementation:
1. Add schemars dependency to swarm-agents Cargo.toml
2. Define JsonSchema-deriving structs for each target type
3. Replace agent.prompt() with agent.extract() where structured output is needed
4. Add fallback parsing for when extraction fails (model returns non-conforming JSON)
5. Test with both Qwen3.5 (local) and cloud models to verify schema compliance

Acceptance: All 5 target types use structured extraction with >95% parse success rate across model tiers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:05Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:05Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-wh8x","title":"Structured extraction via schemars::JsonSchema for typed LLM outputs","description":"Use Rig's structured extraction (agent.extract()) with schemars::JsonSchema derive to force LLMs to return typed Rust structs instead of parsing free-text responses.

Background: Currently the swarm parses LLM text output manually or uses basic JSON parsing. Rig's extract() method generates a JSON schema from Rust types and constrains the LLM to return conforming output, eliminating parsing failures.

Pattern from rig-examples:
```rust
#[derive(Deserialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
struct CodeReview {
    severity: String,
    issues: Vec<Issue>,
    suggestion: String,
    confidence: f64,
}

let review: CodeReview = agent.extract("Review this code: ...").await?;
```

Target types for structured extraction:
1. VerifierResult — pass/fail, error categories, fix suggestions
2. WorkPacketAnalysis — task classification, file contexts, key symbols, complexity score
3. CodeReviewOutput — severity, issues list, suggestions, confidence
4. EscalationDecision — should_escalate, reason, recommended_tier, context summary
5. PlannerOutput — steps list, file targets, dependency graph, risk assessment

Implementation:
1. Add schemars dependency to swarm-agents Cargo.toml
2. Define JsonSchema-deriving structs for each target type
3. Replace agent.prompt() with agent.extract() where structured output is needed
4. Add fallback parsing for when extraction fails (model returns non-conforming JSON)
5. Test with both Qwen3.5 (local) and cloud models to verify schema compliance

Acceptance: All 5 target types use structured extraction with >95% parse success rate across model tiers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:05Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:32:05Z"}

{"id":"beefcake-7v67","title":"Rebuild llama.cpp after PR #19849 merges (qwen35moe chat/thinking fix)","description":"PR #19849 by ggerganov fixes #19690 — hybrid Mamba/attention memory checkpoint issue that causes premature EOS in chat mode with thinking tokens. Once merged, rebuild from HEAD on vasp-01 using the same build process (GCC 12, CUDA 12.6, SM70). The SLURM script already has --jinja with native template, so only a binary swap is needed. Monitor: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/19849","notes":"Root cause confirmed: UD-Q4_K_XL quantization fails instruction following on Qwen3.5-397B-A17B. Tested with --reasoning-format none, enable_thinking=false, plain text format, and /apply-template + completions. All produce garbled output or immediate EOS. Switching to Q4_K_M from lmstudio-community. Download in progress on vasp-02.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T20:20:35Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-27T16:19:03Z"}

old_value: {"id":"beefcake-eao7","title":"Fix: ClientSet ignores coder_endpoint.url (model/URL mismatch)","description":"ClientSet::from_config builds clients.local from fast_endpoint.url only. The general_coder agent uses clients.local + coder_endpoint.model — sending the coder model name to the fast endpoint URL. When fast and coder endpoints are on different servers, this causes a 400 model-not-found error. Fix: build a separate coder_client from coder_endpoint.url in ClientSet, and use it in build_general_coder(). Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs (ClientSet struct + from_config), crates/swarm-agents/src/agents/mod.rs (build_general_coder uses clients.local).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:33:14Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-28T22:33:14Z"}

Fix: build a separate coder_client from coder_endpoint.url in ClientSet, and use it in build_general_coder(). Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs (ClientSet struct + from_config), crates/swarm-agents/src/agents/mod.rs (build_general_coder uses clients.local).","notes":"Dogfood run: 7 iterations over 3.5 hours, 0 code changes. Iter 1 (Worker) hit MaxTurnError at 5 turns — read wrong files, pipe metachar blocked search. Iters 2-7 (Council) manager explored correctly but can't write without cloud. Three quality bugs filed: beefcake-wpp8 (pipe), beefcake-trrf (turn limit), beefcake-0sf0 (catch-22). Issue itself still valid — needs manual fix or re-run after bug fixes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:33:14Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-03-01T08:50:01Z"}
