{"id":"beefcake-noqp","title":"Unblock Qwen3.5-397B chat completions for dogfooding","description":"Apply Codex-recommended debugging steps to fix the Qwen3.5-397B chat completions issue where the model produces flat/random logits after chat template special tokens.

Symptom: /v1/completions works perfectly (sharp logits, coherent output). /v1/chat/completions produces garbage or immediate EOS after processing <|im_start|>, <|im_end|>, <think> tokens. CPU-only reproduces it, ruling out GPU/offload issues.

Codex analysis (2026-02-25): Points to chat-path/template-token/tokenizer-metadata interaction, NOT GPU offloading or quantization.

Debugging steps (priority order):
1. A/B test: render prompt with /apply-template, feed exact string to /v1/completions
   - If works: bug is in chat completions server path (message assembly/prefill/reasoning)
   - If fails: bug is in template tokens / tokenizer metadata / GGUF
2. Try all chat server flags together: --reasoning-format none --reasoning-budget 0 --no-prefill-assistant --no-cache-prompt --cache-reuse 0
3. Compare /tokenize output for <|im_start|> (248045), <|im_end|> (248046), <think> against upstream HF tokenizer for Qwen3.5-MoE — check for GGUF tokenizer metadata mismatch
4. Upgrade llama.cpp past b8145:
   - PR #19849 (merged 2026-02-25): hybrid/recurrent server checkpointing fix (n_past vs position)
   - PR #19866 (merged 2026-02-24): Qwen 3.5 multi-GPU graph split ordering
   - PR #19877 (merged 2026-02-25): re-enable multimodal prompt caching
5. Try a different GGUF source for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (different conversion pipeline)
6. If all else fails: plain-text instruct scaffold avoiding special tokens entirely (System:/User:/Assistant: format via completions endpoint)

Current state:
- vasp-01: Job 1807 running with OLD config (no --jinja, proxy on :8181)
- vasp-02: No SLURM job running
- Chat proxy deployed at /cluster/shared/scripts/llama-cpp/chat-proxy/proxy.py
- Best result so far: 57 tokens with --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking": false}' (garbled quality)
- Tracked upstream: llama.cpp #19690, #19858

4. Structured extraction via schemars::JsonSchema — force LLM to return typed Rust structs for code review output, work packets, verifier results

Depends on: Qwen3.5 inference being functional for dogfooding (sub-epic 6)

Outcomes:
- Concurrent agent sessions share tools cleanly via ToolServer
- Coordination MCP tools accessible from Rig agent context
- Streaming output for real-time progress visibility

- Working Qwen3.5-397B chat completions for local model dogfooding","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:31:19Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-25T22:31:19Z"}

old_value: {"id":"beefcake-qau8","title":"Swarm: model never calls edit_file/write_file (Qwen3-Coder-Next)","description":"Dogfood v2 (PR #18) confirmed all infrastructure fixes work (pipe, turns, tier). ToolChoice::Required forces tool calls but model always picks read/search tools. Root causes to investigate: (1) Model may not understand edit_file/write_file tool schemas (2) Preamble instruction 'MUST call edit_file or write_file' may be too weak (3) Temperature 0.05 may keep model stuck in read-only loop (4) Tool definitions may need more explicit examples showing edit_file usage. old_value: {"id":"beefcake-eao7","title":"Fix: ClientSet ignores coder_endpoint.url (model/URL mismatch)","description":"ClientSet::from_config builds clients.local from fast_endpoint.url only. The general_coder agent uses clients.local + coder_endpoint.model — sending the coder model name to the fast endpoint URL. new_value: {"description":"Add resume-replay guards to ModeOrchestrator (runner.rs:183-246). Currently step() is called unconditionally in a loop with no deduplication. Add: (1) An operation_id (UUID) assigned at each step() call and included in the step result. (2) A seen_operations: HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e in ModeOrchestrator that rejects duplicate operation IDs on restore. (3) A guard in the step loop (runner.rs:208) that skips steps whose operation_id was already applied. (4) When restoring from a checkpoint, populate seen_operations from the checkpoint's step history. Test: create a ModeOrchestrator, run 2 steps, serialize seen_operations, restore, verify step 1 and 2 are skipped but step 3 proceeds. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/runner.rs (ModeOrchestrator at line 165, step loop at line 208), crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/types.rs (add operation_id to ModeOutcome). Do NOT modify contextual.rs or deepthink.rs.","title":"NS-2.5.b: Add resume-replay guards to ModeOrchestrator step loop"}
old_value: {"id":"beefcake-w70b.3.5.2","title":"NS-2.5.b: Add resume replay guards and duplicate-action prevention","description":"Implement safeguards that prevent duplicate tool execution or duplicated iteration counts when resuming from checkpoints after partial progress.","acceptance_criteria":"Resume logic detects already-applied steps and proves idempotent behavior in restart simulations.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:27Z","created_by":"claude-code","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:25:27Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Refactor worker spawn in deepthink.rs to use a reusable agent template instead of building a fresh agent per worker. Currently each worker calls config.local_client().agent().build() inside the spawn block (deepthink.rs:342-375). Change: (1) Build the agent template ONCE before the JoinSet loop (deepthink.rs:145). (2) Clone the template into each spawn (Rig agents should implement Clone -- verify this, and if not, extract the config needed for build into a small AgentTemplate struct that IS Clone). (3) Move the preamble and temperature settings to the template so workers only need to call .prompt(). This reduces per-worker overhead and ensures consistent configuration. Test: existing deepthink tests must still pass. Add a test that spawns 3 workers with the same template and verifies all get the same model/temperature. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/deepthink.rs (spawn loop at line 145, run_strategy_worker at line 342). Do NOT modify runner.rs or agentic.rs.","title":"NS-3.2.a: Refactor deepthink worker spawn to use cloned agent template"}

new_value: {"description":"Improve the join_next collection loop in deepthink.rs. Currently results are collected into a Vec\u003cStrategyOutcome\u003e but there is no streaming accounting or progress reporting. Add: (1) A DeepthinkProgress struct with fields: total_workers, completed, failed, total_elapsed. (2) Emit tracing::info! after each join_next() return with worker index, strategy name, elapsed time, and success/failure. (3) If a worker panics (JoinError), log the panic and record it as a StrategyResult::Failure instead of propagating. (4) Add a timeout per worker (configurable via ModeRunnerConfig, default 5 minutes) using tokio::time::timeout wrapping each spawned task. Test: add a test with a mock worker that sleeps beyond timeout and verify it returns Failure with a timeout message. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/deepthink.rs (join loop after spawn, around line 163+), crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/provider_config.rs (add worker_timeout_secs to ModeRunnerConfig). Do NOT modify contextual.rs or memory.rs.","title":"NS-3.2.b: Add streaming progress and timeout to deepthink join loop"}

new_value: {"description":"Define a DiffBackend trait in apply_diff.rs to abstract the patch application strategy. Currently there is a single inline apply_unified_diff() function (apply_diff.rs:229-305) with no abstraction. Add: (1) A trait DiffBackend with method async fn apply(\u0026self, original: \u0026str, diff: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cPatchResult, DiffError\u003e. (2) Rename the current implementation to InlineDiffBackend that implements this trait. (3) Add a stub GitApplyBackend that shells out to 'git apply --check' then 'git apply' (implementation can return DiffError::Unsupported for now). (4) Document selection criteria in the trait doc comment: InlineDiffBackend for sandbox safety (no git required), GitApplyBackend for production use with fuzzy matching. (5) Update ApplyDiffTool to accept a Box\u003cdyn DiffBackend\u003e and default to InlineDiffBackend. Test: existing apply_diff tests pass with InlineDiffBackend. Add a test for GitApplyBackend that verifies the Unsupported error. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/apply_diff.rs (ApplyDiffTool at line 84, apply_unified_diff at line 229). Do NOT modify agentic.rs directly.","title":"NS-4.3.a: Define DiffBackend trait and refactor apply_diff"}

new_value: {"description":"Add error normalization to DiffBackend implementations in apply_diff.rs. Currently DiffError has variants but they are not consistently mapped across backends. Add: (1) A DiffErrorKind enum with variants: ParseError, ContextMismatch, FileNotFound, PermissionDenied, Timeout, Unsupported, Internal. (2) Add kind() method to DiffError that returns DiffErrorKind. (3) Add is_retryable() method to DiffErrorKind (ContextMismatch and Timeout are retryable, others are not). (4) Ensure InlineDiffBackend maps its errors to correct DiffErrorKind variants. (5) Add context_lines field to DiffError::ContextMismatch showing expected vs actual for debugging. Test: verify DiffErrorKind classification for each error variant. Test is_retryable() returns correct values. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/apply_diff.rs (DiffError enum, InlineDiffBackend). Do NOT modify agentic.rs or runner.rs.","title":"NS-4.3.b: Add DiffError normalization with retryable classification"}

new_value: {"description":"Add explicit invariant checks to memory.rs compaction logic. Currently the compaction function (memory.rs:241-256) reconstructs messages but has no validation guards. Add: (1) A validate_compacted_messages() function that checks: (a) The first message is the system/root context (role=system) and appears exactly once, (b) Recent sliding-window messages are in chronological order (no timestamp inversions), (c) No message appears in both the compacted region and the recent window (splice boundary is clean). (2) Call validate_compacted_messages() after every compaction before returning the result. (3) Return CompactionError::InvariantViolation with a descriptive message if any check fails. (4) Add #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] to CompactionError if not already present. Test: construct a message list where root context is duplicated, verify InvariantViolation. Construct a list where splice boundary overlaps, verify InvariantViolation. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/memory.rs (compaction logic around line 241), crates/swarm-agents/src/modes/types.rs (CompactionSummary at line 178). Do NOT modify contextual.rs or deepthink.rs.","title":"NS-5.4.a: Add compaction invariant validation guards"}

old_value: {"id":"beefcake-qau8","title":"Swarm: model never calls edit_file/write_file (Qwen3-Coder-Next)","description":"Dogfood v2 (PR #18) confirmed all infrastructure fixes work (pipe, turns, tier). ToolChoice::Required forces tool calls but model always picks read/search tools. Root causes to investigate: (1) Model may not understand edit_file/write_file tool schemas (2) Preamble instruction 'MUST call edit_file or write_file' may be too weak (3) Temperature 0.05 may keep model stuck in read-only loop (4) Tool definitions may need more explicit examples showing edit_file usage. This blocks all autonomous code generation with local models.","notes":"Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B MoE specific. Qwen3.5-397B Q4_K_M now running on vasp-02:8081 and expected to have better instruction following at 397B scale. new_value: {"description":"The manager agent has a 45-minute timeout (AGENT_TIMEOUT in orchestrator.rs). When a worker finishes in 8 minutes (observed in job 1629), the manager still runs until timeout because rig's agent-as-tool pattern does not expose intermediate completion signals. Implement option (2) from the original description: monitor the worktree for git changes and short-circuit when changes + verifier pass are detected. Specifically: (1) In orchestrator.rs process_issue(), after spawning the manager agent, start a background tokio::spawn that polls the worktree every 30 seconds for new git commits. (2) When a new commit is detected, run the verifier (cargo fmt + clippy + check + test) on the worktree. (3) If verifier passes, set a CancellationToken that the main agent timeout select\\! branch watches. (4) When the token fires, cancel the agent and proceed to the review phase. (5) Add SWARM_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS env var (default 30) to configure poll frequency. Test: unit test the polling logic with a mock worktree that creates a commit after 2 seconds. Files: crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator.rs (process_issue, AGENT_TIMEOUT), crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs (add poll_interval_secs to SwarmConfig)."}
old_value: {"id":"beefcake-swarm-9fs","title":"Short-circuit manager timeout when worker completes (rig agent opacity)","description":"The manager agent has a 45-minute timeout. Options: (1) Reduce AGENT_TIMEOUT to 15min since most real work happens in first 10min, (2) Monitor worktree for git changes and short-circuit when changes + verifier pass detected, (3) Add a side-channel (file flag) that the verifier sets when green.
